seedData.title: Welcome to Team Wiki
seedData.content:
# Welcome to Team Wiki

This is your team's shared knowledge base. Here you can find answers, how-to guides, and reference documentation.

## Getting Started

1. **Browse** existing articles from the main page
2. **Search** for topics using the search bar
3. **Add** new articles when you learn something worth sharing

## Writing Tips

Articles support **Markdown** formatting:

- Use `# Heading` for section titles
- Use `**bold**` and `*italic*` for emphasis
- Create lists with `-` or `1.`
- Add code with backticks: `like this`

> Share knowledge early and often. A short article today saves hours of questions tomorrow.

seedData.title: How to Request Access
seedData.content:
# How to Request Access

Follow these steps to get access to team resources.

## Steps

1. Open the **IT Portal** at `https://portal.example.com`
2. Click **New Request** and choose *Access Request*
3. Fill in the form:
   - **Resource**: select the system you need
   - **Justification**: explain why you need access
   - **Duration**: choose temporary or permanent
4. Submit and wait for manager approval

## Typical Turnaround

| Request Type | Approval Time |
|---|---|
| Read-only | 1 business day |
| Write access | 2-3 business days |
| Admin access | 5 business days |

## Need Help?

Contact the **IT Help Desk** at ext. 4500 or email `helpdesk@example.com`.

seedData.title: FAQ: Vacation Policy
seedData.content:
# Vacation Policy FAQ

## How many days do I get?

Full-time employees receive **15 days** of PTO per year, plus public holidays.

## How do I request time off?

1. Log into the HR portal
2. Navigate to *Time Off* > *New Request*
3. Select dates and submit

## Can I carry over unused days?

Yes, up to **5 days** can be carried into the next calendar year.

## Who approves my request?

Your direct manager receives an email notification and must approve within **3 business days**.

---

*Last updated: March 2026*

seedData.title: Git Branch Naming Convention
seedData.content:
# Git Branch Naming Convention

All repositories follow this naming scheme:

## Format

```
<type>/<ticket>-<short-description>
```

## Types

- `feature/` â€” new functionality
- `bugfix/` â€” fixing a defect
- `hotfix/` â€” urgent production fix
- `chore/` â€” maintenance tasks

## Examples

- `feature/PROJ-123-user-profile`
- `bugfix/PROJ-456-login-redirect`
- `hotfix/PROJ-789-crash-on-startup`

## Rules

1. Always **lowercase**
2. Use **hyphens** to separate words
3. Keep descriptions under **5 words**
4. Include the **ticket number** from the issue tracker

help.overview: Team Wiki is a shared knowledge base for your team. Write articles in Markdown and browse them with full formatting. Use categories to organize content and the search bar to find articles quickly.

pages.addArticlePage: Enter a title, pick a category, and write your article content using Markdown syntax. Press Publish Article to save.
pages.browseArticlesPage: View all articles as cards. Filter by category or search by keyword. Tap any article to read and edit it.
pages.viewArticlePage: Read the full article with rendered Markdown. Edit the title, category, or content and press Save Changes.

tour.title: Welcome to Team Wiki
tour.content: Build a shared knowledge base with your team. Articles support rich Markdown formatting.

tour.title: Browse Articles
tour.content: All articles appear as cards. Filter by category, search by keyword, or tap any card to read the full article.

tour.title: Write an Article
tour.content: Use Markdown to format your content with headings, lists, code blocks, tables, and more. A live preview shows the rendered result.
